name: ZPU-4 Anti-Aircraft Gun
prompt:
Vietnam War era Soviet ZPU-4 quad anti-aircraft gun. Primary NVA low-altitude air defense weapon.

Physical structure:
- Mount: central pedestal/turntable mount on base
  - Base: flat circular base with leveling jacks
  - Turntable: rotates 360 degrees in azimuth
- Gun cradle: holds 4 x 14.5mm KPV heavy machine gun barrels
  - Barrels arranged in 2x2 square pattern
  - Each barrel: long (~1.5m), thin, with flash hider
  - Shared receiver housing connecting all 4 barrels
  - Elevates on trunnions (can tilt up for anti-aircraft)
- Ammunition: 4 drum magazines, one per barrel
  - Mounted on top/sides of receiver
  - Cylindrical drums
- Sight: mechanical ring sight or optical sight on top
- Seat: gunner's seat behind the cradle, simple metal chair
- Wheels: two rubber-tired wheels on axle for towing (folded up in firing position)
- Trail legs: two outrigger legs folded down for stability
- Shoulder rests: control handles for traversing

Colors: Dark military green (0x3d4a2a), barrel metal (0x333338), rubber wheels (0x222222), brass ammo (0xb5a642)
Orientation: Facing +Z, on ground, barrels slightly elevated
Scale: Real-world meters (~3m deployed width, ~1.5m tall)
animations:
Barrel elevation: cradle tilts up 45 degrees and back over 4 seconds
Turret traverse: base rotates 30 degrees left/right over 6 seconds
